kept.text:
It is with a heavy heart that I must announce that this Frank Gehry house in Thousand Oaks, CA, known as the Sirmai–Peterson House (1988) was sold for $2,450,000 before it could ever hit the market. 

But at least we can look at the photos and dream about the life we could have had. 

However, I was told that the new stewards are “thoughtful and committed, and we expect the house will be exceptionally well cared for moving forward”.

follow me @samir
Play the Zillow gone wild game, Househunt!! 👇👇👇

kept.text:
ANYONE could make $20k per month wholesaling deals off Zillow working less than 4 hours per day. Here’s how:

1. Pick a major metro (ideally your back yard). You will want to target not only the city itself but also every county that boarders it. You may even want to also target the counties that boarder the counties that boarder the city.
2. Search for homes for sale in as many counties that Zillow lets you do at once.
3. Filter the homes so that you only see single families, townhomes, or multifamily. Make sure you exclude all new construction, foreclosures or bank owned, and for sale by owners (we only want agent listed).

4. Put the properties in list view (default is map view I believe) and then sort the list from lowest price to highest price. We will be making offers on ALL properties that need repairs, and all occupied rentals.

5. Click a property, go to the bottom of the property description and you will see who the property is listed by. Copy and paste the realtor’s name into https://t.co/782eRBKXiF and you should be able to quickly find the agents cell phone.

6. Text the agent this: Hi (agent name), my name is (your first and last name), and I am an investor interested in your listing at (property address). Would (50%-60% of asking price - or if you know how to comp then give a real lowball) work for your seller if I am cash, but it as is, and close quick?

7. They will say no. Once they say no, respond like this: Fair enough! How much higher would I need to be at?

8. If they say you need to be at or around asking, tell them you wouldn’t be able to sadly, but ask if hey have any other deals currently or coming up. If they are nice then save them to a list of nice agents 

9. If they come back with a price that is at least 20%-30% discount from asking, then you have yourself a motivated seller lead. It is now time to really comp the property or bring he deal to an experienced wholesaler/flipper who can comp it for you.

10. You’re not making the initial offers to get deals, you make them to uncover motivated sellers. Uncover enough motivated sellers and you will get them convert. Every 100 offers should bring you 1 deal roughly, with an average wholesale fee of $10k

11. When you finally find a deal that works, the realtor will send you the contract to sign. Sign it. If they ask for proof of fund and you are broke then go to https://t.co/vJa9TRslii and get free POF in seconds.

12. Now that you have the deal, have an experienced wholesaler sell it for you. If you already know how to dispo it then dispo it yourself and keep all the bread. 

13. 10 offers per day is 300 offers. This is enough to get AT LEAST 3 deals under contract. Likely 2 of those deals will close each month.

14. Follow up the next day with anyone who doesn’t respond to your initial offer.

15. Once a month text everyone on your nice agent list and ask if they have any deals coming up. Overtime this list will become very valuable as it grows.

16. Happy hunting

quoted_tweet.text:
🚨🚨🚨 $400,000,000!!!!!

Yes you read that right!! Here it is friends!! The most expensive home we’ve ever listed! The crown jewel of Los Angeles!! 

This home took a decade to build and was completed in 2018 on 8 acres of mostly flat space with views of downtown to the pacific Ocean.

There is over 70,000 sq ft across multiple structures and is regarded as “the most expensive home in the USA (what better way to celebrate it’s 250 year anni than buying this). 

The main house has 10 bedrooms, 13 staff bedrooms and the guest house has 6 guest bedrooms with 10 more staff bedrooms. 

Currently listed for only $400 million.

With an end of the year move coming up I have been looking at a lot of real estate in middle Tennessee outside Nashville. The big question is to rent or to buy, I am leaning towards renting a few more years. Keep saving and buy an even better home for my family.

The monthly payments are similar but purchasing something requires me putting 20% down which in this hypothetical scenario is like $91k which I could just leave in say $STRC making 11% ... idk what do you guys think?
